news.headline: Strait of Hormuz Crisis: US-Iran Resume Fighting, Strait Effectively Closed
news.what_happened: The IRGC Navy declared the Strait of Hormuz closed on July 12 after firing warning shots and striking the Cyprus-flagged container ship GFS Galaxy. No large vessel has crossed since Tuesday. The US reimposed oil sanctions on Iran and struck 80+ targets across Iran. The Strait handles approximately 20% of global oil and gas trade. Oman is mediating with a proposal for two separately controlled routes.
news.why_it_matters: This is the most severe oil supply disruption in history. India imports 85% of its crude oil needs, with significant volumes via the Strait. Higher crude widens India's current account deficit, pressures the INR, and elevates inflation risk. ONGC and OIL India see direct revenue surge from higher crude realization. Airlines, paints, tyres, chemicals, and logistics face severe margin compression.

news.headline: WTI Crude +4.33% to $74.50/bbl; Brent +4.26% to $79.25 — Largest Single-Week Jump in Years
news.what_happened: WTI crude oil surged from $71.41 to $74.50 (+4.33%) and Brent from $76.01 to $79.25 (+4.26%) as geopolitical risk premium spiked on the Hormuz closure. The move was amplified by US reimposing Iranian oil sanctions.
news.why_it_matters: Jet fuel (35-40% of airline OPEX) costs surge; every $1 crude rise costs IndiGo ~₹400 Cr/year. Paint raw materials, synthetic rubber, carbon black all rise in tandem. Upstream oil producers ONGC and OIL see near-direct revenue upside.

news.headline: FII Net Buyers ₹2,603 Cr + DII Buyers ₹2,019 Cr — Strong Dual Institutional Flows
news.what_happened: Foreign Portfolio Investors (FIIs) net bought ₹2,603.70 Cr in Indian equities on Friday. Domestic institutions (DII) simultaneously net bought ₹2,019.70 Cr. Both categories buying together is rare and a strong support signal.
news.why_it_matters: When FII and DII both buy at scale, Nifty large caps receive double-sided support. HDFCBANK (highest FII ownership ~55%), ICICIBANK, RELIANCE, INFY, TCS are typical FII priority targets. DII buying also supports mid-cap PSUs.

news.headline: US S&P 500 +0.42%, Nasdaq +0.29% — US Markets End Week Positive Despite Oil Shock
news.what_happened: S&P 500 closed at 7,575 (+0.42%) and Nasdaq at 26,281 (+0.29%) on Friday. SK Hynix completed its $26.5B US listing — the largest-ever by a foreign company — contributing to positive sentiment.
news.why_it_matters: Positive global close supports risk-on cue for Indian markets on Monday open. FII flows likely to continue. Banking and IT large caps benefit from the international tailwind, partially offsetting the crude headwind.

news.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate at 5.25% — Neutral Stance; GDP Growth 6.9%, CPI 4.6%
news.what_happened: RBI MPC unanimously held the repo rate at 5.25% with a neutral stance. GDP growth forecast for FY27 is 6.9%, CPI inflation projected at 4.6%. RBI also unveiled plans for e-Kuber 3.0 and a governance amendment review.
news.why_it_matters: No rate cut surprise means banking NIMs are stable. If crude-driven inflation pushes CPI above 4.6%, RBI will face pressure to stay hawkish longer — headwind for rate-sensitive sectors (NBFCs, real estate).

news.headline: China PMI 50.3 in June — Mild Expansion Led by High-Tech, AI Demand
news.what_happened: China's official PMI rose to 50.3 from 50.0, beating the 50.1 forecast. High-tech manufacturing PMI surged to 53.5 on AI investment demand. Consumer goods PMI lagged at 50.2. Non-manufacturing PMI at 50.2.
news.why_it_matters: Mild positive for metals demand. TATASTEEL, HINDALCO, VEDL get a marginal tailwind. However, China's growth is tech-led, not commodity-led, limiting the metals rally catalyst.

news.headline: ONGC Approves 1.75 MT Strategic Oil Reserve Expansion at Mangaluru
news.what_happened: ONGC approved expansion of India's strategic petroleum reserve at Mangaluru by 1.75 million tonnes as the government urgently strengthens energy security following the Hormuz supply shock.
news.why_it_matters: Signals government's commitment to ONGC as a strategic priority. Positive for ONGC's long-term capex pipeline and government backing. Reinforces the bullish thesis on crude-exposed upstream stocks.

sector_outlook.reason: Crude WTI +4.33%, Brent +4.26%; Strait of Hormuz closure sustains elevated prices; ONGC/OIL revenue surges with every dollar of crude rise
FII net buying ₹2,603 Cr; GIFT Nifty gap-up +0.55%; S&P +0.42%; HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, KOTAKBANK in FII buy zone
Geopolitical tensions (US-Iran war) drive defense sentiment; HAL, BEL, BEML, COCHINSHIP in focus
USD/INR flat at 95.37; Nasdaq only +0.29% (below 1% threshold); no strong INR/Nasdaq catalyst today
China PMI mild at 50.3; AI-led demand not broad commodity demand; TATASTEEL, HINDALCO mild tailwind only
Short-term inventory gains from crude rise; long-term under-recovery risk if govt delays retail price hike
Crude +4.33% is devastating — jet fuel is 35-40% of IndiGo OPEX; every $1 crude rise = ₹400 Cr annual cost; INDIGO, SPICEJET in deep trouble
Crude-derived raw materials (TiO2, monomers) = 40-50% of paint costs; ASIANPAINT, BERGEPAINT, KANSAINER face margin squeeze
Synthetic rubber and carbon black (crude derivatives) = 60-70% of input costs; MRF, APOLLOTYRE, CEATLTD, BALKRISIND under pressure
Diesel/fuel costs surge with crude; DELHIVERY, BLUEDART, CONCOR OPEX worsens
Higher fuel prices hit consumer sentiment on vehicle purchases; MARUTI, TATAMOTORS, M&M face volume risk
Petroleum-based feedstock prices surge; PIDILITIND, DEEPAKFERT, GNFC face raw material cost inflation

picks.catalyst: WTI Crude +4.33% ($74.50/bbl); Strait of Hormuz closure sustains elevated crude prices; ONGC strategic reserve expansion approved
picks.logic: ONGC is India's largest upstream oil producer, realizing approximately market crude prices on its output. Every $1 sustained increase in crude = ~₹350-400 Cr annual PAT impact. The Strait of Hormuz closure is not a one-day event — supply risk premium likely persists for weeks. ONGC also received government approval for strategic reserve expansion at Mangaluru, signaling strong policy priority. Purest direct crude-to-P&L beneficiary on NSE with no hedging constraints.

picks.catalyst: WTI Crude +4.33%; OIL India is India's 2nd-largest upstream crude & natural gas producer with direct crude price realization
picks.logic: Oil India Limited revenue is directly tied to crude realization. At $74.50/bbl vs. a break-even around $40-45, every additional dollar flows near-directly to operating profit. Higher beta to crude than ONGC due to smaller market cap = larger % move expected. Geopolitical risk premium from Hormuz crisis likely sustains for 1-2+ weeks. Government entity with full crude price pass-through to revenue.

picks.catalyst: FII net buying ₹2,603 Cr; GIFT Nifty +0.55% gap-up; S&P 500 +0.42%; dual institutional buying (FII+DII both net buyers)
picks.logic: HDFCBANK has the highest FII ownership (~55%) of any major Indian bank. When FIIs are net buyers at ₹2,600+ Cr scale, HDFCBANK captures the largest inflow share by index weight. GIFT Nifty signals a positive open. Credit growth stable at ~13% with RBI rate on hold (NIM compression risk contained). Classic FII-driven rally setup — strongest large-cap bank pick on institutional flow days.

risks: Strait of Hormuz escalation: if crude WTI breaches $80, India macro deteriorates sharply — CAD widens, INR pressured, RBI stays hawkish; broad market rally stalls
Nifty gap-up 1%+: stocks may already price in positives at open; wait for watch zones before entry — don't chase the gap
Monsoon 2026 below normal: rural demand stress weighing on FMCG, fertilizers, rural banking stocks throughout the session
US 10Y yield rising at 4.569%: sustained yield rise reduces FII appetite for EMs; risk of sudden flow reversal
Airlines/Paints/Tyres expected to underperform severely — INDIGO, ASIANPAINT, MRF likely to fall; avoid long positions in these sectors today
eod_summary: Muted day despite crude tailwind: ONGC +0.87%, OIL +0.61%, HDFCBANK +0.12%. All 3 picks underperformed targets — stocks opened near watch-zone highs and lacked follow-through. Nifty 50 +0.71% to 24,211. 0/3 picks hit target.
